title: Berlin Hop-On Hop-Off Bus - Quick Summary
items: **The hop-on hop-off bus is convenient, not essential.** Central Berlin's headline sights sit in a tight walkable line, and the normal Bus 100/200 already covers most of the same route. Decide by your situation, not by habit.

**What it costs in 2026.** Roughly **€30–€45 per adult for a 24-hour pass** across Berlin's four main operators, with children reduced. For comparison, a BVG day ticket is €11.20 and a single ride is €4.00.
**The buses stop early.** Most run about **09:30 to 18:00** and thin out in winter, so a 24-hour pass is really a daytime pass. Your own feet and the normal buses keep going all evening.

**The cheap local alternative is Bus 100/200.** These normal double-deckers run past the Reichstag, Brandenburg Gate and Tiergarten between Alexanderplatz and Zoologischer Garten. A top-front seat gives you the sightseeing view at day-ticket price.

**When the paid bus is genuinely worth it.** Limited walking, a short trip where you want wide coverage fast, cold or wet weather, or a family that wants a seated base with recorded commentary in their own language.

**My default advice.** Start with a tip-based walking tour to get the story and your bearings, walk the centre, and save Bus 100/200 for the one longer stretch. Keep the sightseeing-bus money for food.
